name: jira
description: Jira Cloud integration for issue management and search. This skill should
capabilities: Retrieve issue details with GET requests to REST API v3
Search issues using JQL with filtering and sorting
Create and update issues with JSON payload
Add comments using Atlassian Document Format
Transition issue status through workflow states
Assign and unassign issues to team members
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Jira Cloud integration skill. All 312 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown documentation code blocks as executable code. The Python script uses standard HTTP Basic Auth (base64 encoding) and urllib for API requests - no command injection or credential exfiltration risks exist.
valueStatement: Automate Jira Cloud workflows by searching issues with JQL queries, creating and updating tickets, adding comments, and transitioning status. This skill provides direct access to REST API v3 for seamless integration.
userTitle: Manage Jira issues with JQL and REST API
limitations: Does not support Jira Server (on-premise) instances
Rate limits apply per Atlassian API constraints

useCases.title: Track development work
useCases.description: Search assigned issues, update ticket status, and log progress directly from Claude Code

useCases.title: Monitor project status
useCases.description: Query sprint progress, bulk retrieve issue details, and generate status reports via JQL

useCases.title: Automate ticket workflows
useCases.description: Create incidents from alerts, transition issues to resolved, and sync status across systems

promptTemplates.title: Get issue details
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the details for issue PROJ-123 including status, assignee, and priority

promptTemplates.title: Search my issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all my open issues in project AOP that were updated in the last day

promptTemplates.title: Create issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new bug issue in PROJ with summary 'Login fails on mobile' and description 'User cannot login using Safari browser'

promptTemplates.title: Bulk status update
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all issues in sprint 'Sprint 23' assigned to me and transition them to In Review

bestPractices: Store Jira credentials in environment variables or .env file, never in code
Use JQL functions like currentUser() for personalized queries
Handle rate limits by adding delays between bulk operations
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode API tokens or email in script files
Do not use personal credentials for automated workflows - use service accounts
Do not expose Jira URLs or credentials in logs or error messages

faq.question: How do I generate a Jira API token?
faq.answer: Go to https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens and create a new token.

faq.question: Can this skill work with self-hosted Jira?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed for Jira Cloud only. It requires a domain like company.atlassian.net.

faq.question: What authentication does this skill use?
faq.answer: HTTP Basic Auth with email and API token, encoded in base64 for the Authorization header.

faq.question: How do I search for my assigned issues?
faq.answer: Use JQL: assignee = currentUser() AND resolution = Unresolved ORDER BY updated DESC

faq.question: What is Atlassian Document Format?
faq.answer: ADF is Jira's JSON-based format for rich text. Use it for descriptions and comments with formatting.

faq.question: Does this skill support Jira automation rules?
faq.answer: No, this skill interacts with the REST API directly. Use Jira's native automation for automated workflows.
